unmet_needs_summary.description: Users find it awkward to 'nag' partners; desire for automated nudges that don't feel like policing.
Couples have different schedules/stamina; apps fail to balance 'shared goal' vs 'individual contribution'.
Lack of 'micro-celebrations' or low-friction ways to acknowledge a partner's progress without a full text message.
Difficulty in choosing which personal habits to keep private vs. which to share with a partner.
Existing apps are too 'RPG' or 'childish'; desire for a clean, adult aesthetic for shared living.

raw_data_samples.comment: I want to track gym visits with my wife, but if I miss a day and she doesn't, I feel like a failure and she feels like a nag for asking if I went. We need a way to see each other's progress without the verbal friction.
The party system is okay, but it's too much for just a couple. I don't want to fight monsters, I just want to know if my boyfriend actually did the dishes like we agreed.
Most habit trackers are single-player. The 'social' ones are too public. There's a middle ground for high-trust duos where visibility is the primary motivator.
We tried using a shared Google Sheet for our 'health month' but updating it is a chore. I wish I could just get a notification when she finishes her run so I can send a quick emoji cheer.
Great app, but why can't I link my streak to my partner's? We are training for a marathon together. If she fails, our 'team streak' should feel it, but I shouldn't lose my personal progress.
The hardest part of shared habits is that I work nights and he works days. We need a dashboard that accounts for our different 'days' while keeping a shared goal of 1500 calories.
Shared habit apps always turn into a blame game. I want an app that focuses on the 'we'—how are we doing as a unit? If I slack, how can she pick up the slack without it being a fight?
I want to share my 'reading' habit with my partner but definitely not my 'no-porn' habit. Apps currently are all-or-nothing on sharing.
The community aspect is too broad. I only care about one person's opinion. Give me a 1-on-1 habit interface.
It's the small wins. Just a 'nudge' or a 'high five' button inside the app would save us so many annoying 'did you do it?' texts.
